---
title: "Viewer"
---

# Viewer

AutoDesk provides a WebGL-based viewer for rendering 2D and 3D models. Before using the viewer, translate the file into SVF format and extract its encoded URN — see the **Model Derivative** chapter for those steps.

## Launch the Viewer

Pass the encoded URN and token to `viewer3D()` to open the viewer as a Shiny app. The `viewerType` parameter controls the viewer UI:

| `viewerType` | Description |
|---|---|
| `"header"` | Full viewer with toolbar and side panels (default) |
| `"headless"` | Viewer canvas only — no toolbar or panels |
| `"vr"` | WebVR mode optimized for mobile devices |

```{r}
#| eval: false
resp <- getToken(
  id     = Sys.getenv("client_id"),
  secret = Sys.getenv("client_secret"),
  scope  = "data:read"
)
myToken      <- resp$content$access_token
myEncodedUrn <- jsonlite::base64_enc(Sys.getenv("urn"))

# Full viewer (default)
viewer3D(urn = myEncodedUrn, token = myToken)

# Headless — embed in a dashboard without chrome
viewer3D(urn = myEncodedUrn, token = myToken, viewerType = "headless")

# VR mode for mobile
viewer3D(urn = myEncodedUrn, token = myToken, viewerType = "vr")
```

<img src="https://github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR/blob/master/inst/images/aerial.png?raw=true" height="500px" />

## Embed in a Shiny App

Use `viewerUI()` to embed the viewer inside any Shiny application via the Shiny Modules framework. The `id` parameter namespaces the module so multiple viewers can coexist on the same page.

```{r}
#| eval: false
ui <- function(request) {
  shiny::fluidPage(
    viewerUI("viewer1", urn = myEncodedUrn, token = myToken)
  )
}

server <- function(input, output, session) {}

shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)
```

To show a headless viewer inside a `tabsetPanel`:

```{r}
#| eval: false
ui <- function(request) {
  shiny::fluidPage(
    shiny::tabsetPanel(
      shiny::tabPanel("Model", viewerUI("viewer1", myEncodedUrn, myToken, viewerType = "headless")),
      shiny::tabPanel("Data",  shiny::verbatimTextOutput("meta"))
    )
  )
}

server <- function(input, output, session) {}

shiny::shinyApp(ui, server)
```
